title: Vendor Risk Assessment Due Dates for Critical Suppliers

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-013-1 R1 Part 1.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-013-1 R1 Part 1.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.64: Vendor Risk Assessment Due Dates for Critical Suppliers.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.64' running on index=grc sourcetype=vendor:risk, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype=vendor:risk critical_vendor=1 earliest=-730d
| eval due=strptime(assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_left=round((due-now())/86400,0)
| where days_left < 30 OR isnull(assessment_completed)
| table vendor_id, vendor_name, assessment_due, days_left, owner
| sort days_left
description: We track vendor security risk assessments for suppliers touching BES Cyber Systems and alert before assessments expire.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes vendor:risk, vendor:risk`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Sync vendor register from TPRM tool. (2) Alert at ninety, thirty, and seven days. (3) Block new access for expired high-risk vendors per policy. (4) Export for supply chain committee. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=vendor:risk`, `sourcetype=vendor:risk``.
visualization: Table (due soon), Single value (expired count), Timeline

grandmaExplanation: We watch for we track vendor security risk assessments for suppliers touching bes cyber systems and alert before assessments expire. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype=vendor:risk`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Sync vendor register from TPRM tool. (2) Alert at ninety, thirty, and seven days. (3) Block new access for expired high-risk vendors per policy. (4) Export for supply chain committee.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=vendor:risk critical_vendor=1 earliest=-730d
| eval due=strptime(assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_left=round((due-now())/86400,0)
| where days_left < 30 OR isnull(assessment_completed)
| table vendor_id, vendor_name, assessment_due, days_left, owner
| sort days_left
```

Understanding this SPL

**Vendor Risk Assessment Due Dates for Critical Suppliers (CIP-013-1 R1 Part 1.1)** — We track vendor security risk assessments for suppliers touching BES Cyber Systems and alert before assessments expire.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype=vendor:risk`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: vendor:risk. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=vendor:risk, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_left** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_left < 30 OR isnull(assessment_completed)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Output fields: assessment_due, days_left, due, owner, vendor_id, vendor_name.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (due soon), Single value (expired count), Timeline
